task: I’m trying to put together a budget-conscious outfit shortlist and want it to feel like a real shopping comparison, not just random picks. Start on Depop and go to the seller page for rainbow_bebe to see whether they currently have any prom dress listings under $100, because if there’s a good one there I’d use that as my budget anchor; open at least one qualifying listing in its own tab so I can see the actual photos and price on the live listing page. If that seller doesn’t have a convincing under-$100 option, switch to PrettyLittleThing and find an emerald green dress that’s available in size 12, then open the product page and note the name and price so I have a fallback main dress candidate. After that, on JJ’s House, browse cocktail dresses and find one deep V-neck option that looks like a dressier comparison point, and keep that product page open too so I can compare the styling. Once you’ve seen those dress options, decide which dress feels like the best value based on price and what’s actually available, then go to Old Navy and pick one comfortable-looking, affordable women’s jogger to round out the shortlist as a casual extra, making sure you open the actual product page and capture the name and price. Then check Tecovas for one men’s cowboy boot made of real leather, open the product page, and list the design or color options shown there so I can see how many choices there are. Finally, go to ALS and look up the Nike Phantom 6 Club FG/MG low-top men’s soccer cleat, tell me the current price, and say clearly whether it stays under $60 so I can judge whether this whole shortlist still feels budget-friendly. Please leave the key product tabs open for the dress comparison and give me a final shortlist with the item names, prices, the Tecovas options, and a quick note on which dress you’d personally pick as the best value.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Depop seller page for `rainbow_bebe` (Step 6). It then filtered the shop for "Dresses" (Step 16) and opened a specific listing for a "purple black lace mini dress" (Step 17). The agent confirmed the price of this item was $82.60 (which is under $100) and kept the tab open for comparison (Step 19). The agent also explicitly noted in its final summary that this was an under-$100 option, though it ultimately deemed it less "convincing" as a prom anchor than the other options found later. The requirement to check the seller, find a qualifying listing, and capture the title and price is satisfied.

R2: success - The agent successfully navigated to PrettyLittleThing, searched for an emerald green dress, and confirmed that size 12 was available (as seen in step 32, where the agent selected size 12 and the page updated to show "Size: 12" and "Only 3 left in stock"). The product name ("Plus Emerald Green Chiffon One Shoulder Ruched Detail Maxi Dress") and price ($33.50) were recorded in the final summary.

R3: success - The agent successfully identified a JJ's House cocktail dress ("Ermira Chiffon Midi Cocktail Dress") that features a V-neck design. The product page was opened (tab 2 in the final state), and the name and price ($79) were captured and included in the final summary. The agent also explicitly kept the dress comparison tabs open as requested.

R4: success - The agent successfully selected the PrettyLittleThing dress as the best-value pick in the final summary (Step 62). It provided a clear rationale, noting that it is a "full maxi-style emerald dress," "actually available in size 12," and "much cheaper than the Depop and JJ’s House options." This satisfies the requirement to select one dress as the best-value pick and provide a brief comparison note.

R5: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Old Navy website, searched for women's joggers, and opened the product page for the "High-Waisted PowerSoft Pocket Joggers". The agent recorded the name ("High-Waisted PowerSoft Pocket Joggers") and the price ($23.99) in the final summary, which matches the product page details captured during the navigation.

R6: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Tecovas product page for "The Buck" (Step 55). The page confirms the material is "cowhide" (real leather). The agent correctly identified and listed the four color/design options (Light Tobacco, Molasses, Dark Bourbon, and Copper) in the final summary (Step 62).

R7: success - The agent successfully navigated to the ALS product page for the "Nike Phantom 6 Club FG/MG Low-Top Soccer Cleat - Men's" (Step 59). The agent recorded the price as $48.99 in the final summary (Step 62) and explicitly stated that it stays under $60. The verification requirement is met as the product title and price are clearly identified in the trajectory.

R8: success - The agent successfully consolidated all required items into a final shortlist in the last step.
- Dress results: Included Depop, PrettyLittleThing, and JJ's House.
- Best-value dress: Explicitly identified the PrettyLittleThing dress as the best value.
- Old Navy jogger: Included name ("High-Waisted PowerSoft Pocket Joggers") and price ($23.99).
- Tecovas boot: Included name ("The Buck") and listed the four color options (Light Tobacco, Molasses, Dark Bourbon, Copper).
- ALS cleat: Included name and price ($48.99) and confirmed it is under $60.
The final response is coherent and includes all requested information.
